2604.13993.title: Reward Design for Physical Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
2604.13993.paper_summary: Physical reasoning over visual inputs demands tight integration of visual perception, domain knowledge, and multi-step symbolic inference. Yet even state-of-the-art Vision Language Models (VLMs) fall far short of human performance on physics benchmarks. While post-training algorithms such as Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT) and Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) have demonstrated strong reasoning gains in language models, how reward design shapes VLM physical reasoning behavior remains poorly understood. We present a systematic reward ablation study for GRPO-based VLM training on physical reasoning. We compare four reward signals of increasing semantic richness: format compliance, answer accuracy, a composite rubric reward (answer correctness, physics principle identification, and unit consistency), and a novel internal reward derived from model attention weights over input image regions. We evaluate on PhyX, a 3,000-problem benchmark spanning six physics domains and six reasoning types across multiple-choice and open-ended formats, using IBM Granite Vision 3.3 (2B). Across both formats, GRPO with accuracy-based rewards outperforms SFT on most domains, though gains vary substantially by reward type and domain. Reward design does not uniformly improve performance. Instead, it induces domain-specific reasoning behaviors. Accuracy-based rewards provide the strongest overall gains. Rubric rewards improve structured reasoning quality without consistent accuracy improvements. Attention-based rewards enhance spatial reasoning while degrading performance in symbolic domains. Our internal attention-weight reward requires no spatial annotations and improves spatial relation accuracy from 0.27 to 0.50, suggesting that supervising where the model attends during generation is a promising direction for visually grounded physical reasoning.
2604.13993.paper_summary_zh: 物理推理對視覺輸入的要求需要視覺感知、領域知識和多步驟符號推理的緊密整合。<lb/>然而，即使是最先進的視覺語言模型（VLMs）在物理基準測試中的表現仍遠遠不及人類。<lb/>儘管後訓練算法如監督微調（SFT）和群體相對策略優化（GRPO）在語言模型中顯示出強大的推理增益，但獎勵設計如何塑造VLM的物理推理行為仍然不甚了解。<lb/>我們針對基於GRPO的VLM訓練進行了一項系統的獎勵消融研究，專注於物理推理。<lb/>我們比較了四種語義豐富度逐漸增加的獎勵信號：格式合規性、答案準確性、一個綜合評分獎勵（答案正確性、物理原理識別和單位一致性），以及一個從模型注意權重衍生的創新內部獎勵，該獎勵針對輸入圖像區域。<lb/>我們在PhyX上進行評估，這是一個涵蓋六個物理領域和六種類型推理的3,000題基準測試，並使用IBM Granite Vision 3.3（2B）。<lb/>在這兩種格式中，基於準確性的GRPO在大多數領域的表現優於SFT，儘管增益因獎勵類型和領域而異。<lb/>獎勵設計並不均勻地改善性能。<lb/>相反，它引發了特定領域的推理行為。<lb/>基於準確性的獎勵提供了最強的整體增益。<lb/>評分獎勵改善了結構化推理的質量，但未能一致提高準確性。<lb/>基於注意力的獎勵增強了空間推理，但在符號領域中表現下降。<lb/>我們的內部注意權重獎勵不需要空間標註，並將空間關係的準確性從0.27提高到0.50，這表明在生成過程中監督模型的注意力位置是一個有前景的方向，用於視覺基礎的物理推理。
2604.13993.author: Derek Lilienthal et.al.
2604.13993.authors: Derek Lilienthal, Manisha Mukherjee, Sameera Horawalavithana
